title: Ensure that the --kubelet-https argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --kubelet-https argument is set to true
input_row.rationale: Connections from apiserver to kubelets could potentially carry sensitive data such as secrets and keys. It is thus important to use in-transit encryption for any communication between the apiserver and kubelets.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --kubelet-https argument either does not exist or is set to true .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and remove the --kubelet-https parameter.
input_row.impact: You require TLS to be configured on apiserver as well as kubelets.
input_row.default_value: By default, kubelet connections are over https.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_kubelet_https_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the command-line arguments of the kube-apiserver.
- The specific command to run is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver pods.
- The expected configuration property to validate is the presence of the `--kubelet-https=true` argument.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `--kubelet-https` argument is either set to `true` or does not exist.
  - **Fail:** The `--kubelet-https` argument is set to `false`.
